questions.question: What is the Acyclic Dependencies Principle and how do you break a cycle?
questions.expectedAnswer: ADP states that the package dependency graph must have no cycles. Break cycles by applying DIP (inverting a dependency so one package depends on an interface rather than the concrete other package) or by extracting the shared dependency into a new package.
questions.rubric: Must state no-cycles rule and at least one technique for breaking cycles

questions.question: What is the relationship between CCP and SRP?
questions.expectedAnswer: CCP is SRP at the package level. Just as SRP says a class should have one reason to change, CCP says classes that change for the same reason should be in the same package. A change should affect one package, not many.
questions.rubric: Must explicitly connect CCP to SRP and explain the package-level application
